name: Elavon Payment Processing API
what_it_does: Elavon enterprise payment processing REST API for large merchants in hospitality, healthcare, retail, and professional services to accept card payments, manage payment security, process EMV chip and contactless transactions, and handle industry-specific payment workflows. Enables AI agents to manage payment transaction authorization and capture for enterprise payment automation, handle card tokenization and omni-token for secure payment storage automation, access fraud management and 3-D Secure for payment security automation, retrieve settlement and funding report for financial reconciliation automation, manage recurring payment and subscription billing for enterprise subscription automation, handle refund and chargeback management for dispute resolution automation, access multi-currency and international payment processing for global commerce automation, retrieve industry-specific payment features (hospitality: folio, healthcare: FSA/HSA) for vertical payment automation, manage partner and ISO program management for payment channel automation, and integrate Elavon with Salesforce, SAP, and enterprise ERP for end-to-end enterprise payment management.
use_cases: Processing hospitality payments from hotel property management agents
Accepting healthcare payments from HSA/FSA billing agents
Managing enterprise card transactions from payment orchestration agents
Handling EMV and contactless from retail POS automation agents
not_for: Startup and developer-first payments (use Stripe for modern developer experience)
Global e-commerce marketplaces (use Adyen or Stripe Connect for marketplace)
LATAM or APAC regional payments (use local processors for non-US/EU markets)
best_when: A large enterprise in hospitality, healthcare, or retail wanting AI agents to automate payment processing, omni-channel tokenization, industry-specific payment features, and multi-currency settlement through Elavon's US Bancorp-backed enterprise payment platform.
avoid_when: ENTERPRISE IMPLEMENTATION REQUIREMENT: Elavon enterprise onboarding requires dedicated relationship manager and implementation engagement; automated self-service Elavon integration is not the intended model; automated standalone integration without implementation support creates gaps in merchant account configuration. US BANK RELATIONSHIP FOR ENTERPRISE ACCOUNTS: Elavon is a US Bank subsidiary; enterprise merchant accounts may require US Bank relationship; automated merchant onboarding requires coordination with Elavon merchant services team. INDUSTRY-SPECIFIC FEATURES REQUIRE VERTICAL CONFIGURATION: Elavon's hospitality, healthcare, and retail features require account-level vertical configuration; automated industry-specific payment workflow must be configured during merchant onboarding; automated folio or FSA/HSA features without vertical configuration creates unsupported feature errors.

auth.notes: Elavon uses API key and OAuth for integrations. REST API with JSON. Atlanta, Georgia HQ. Founded 1991. US Bank subsidiary. Products: Payment gateway, tokenization, EMV, contactless, recurring billing, hospitality/healthcare verticals. SDKs: Java, .NET, PHP. PCI DSS Level 1. Serves 1.5M+ global merchants. Competes with Worldpay, Heartland, and Chase Paymentech for enterprise payment processing.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — enterprise merchant contract required
pricing.paid_tiers: Custom enterprise pricing based on volume and industry; typically interchange-plus for large merchants
pricing.notes: Atlanta GA. US Bank subsidiary. Custom enterprise pricing. Annual contract. Implementation engagement.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per merchant API key; transaction processing throttled; webhooks for payment and dispute events

known_agent_gotchas: OMNI TOKEN vs CARD TOKEN vs NETWORK TOKEN TYPES: Elavon's Converge platform uses multiple token types — Omni-Token (cross-channel), Card Token (single-channel), and Network Token (card network token); automated tokenization strategy must select appropriate token type for use case; automated Omni-Token for single-channel use adds unnecessary token management overhead

CONVERGE vs FUSEBOX vs SIMPLIFY PRODUCT LINE APIS: Elavon has multiple product APIs (Converge for hosted, FuseBox for direct integration, Simplify for payment buttons); automated integration must target correct Elavon product; automated cross-product API calls create endpoint routing errors

HOSPITALITY FOLIO MANAGEMENT REQUIRES PROPERTY MANAGEMENT SYSTEM INTEGRATION: Elavon hospitality folio features require PMS (Property Management System) integration configuration; automated hotel payment workflow must coordinate PMS and Elavon folio endpoints; automated hotel payment without PMS integration creates incomplete folio with missing stay details

SETTLEMENT BATCH TIMING FOR RECONCILIATION: Elavon batch settlement timing varies by merchant configuration (some automatic, some manual batch close); automated reconciliation must understand batch configuration; automated same-day reconciliation for manual-batch merchants requires triggering batch close before reconciliation

CHARGEBACK RESPONSE DOCUMENTATION FORMAT: Elavon chargeback responses require specific documentation format per card network; automated chargeback management must format evidence according to card network requirements (Visa vs Mastercard vs AMEX); automated generic evidence response format creates rejected dispute regardless of evidence quality
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise payments. PCI-DSS Level 1. API key/OAuth. US/EU. Payment and enterprise data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise payments. PCI-DSS Level 1. API key/OAuth. US/EU. Payment data.
